description:
Enhance the CSS Architecture analyzer (src/handlers/analyzers/css-architecture.ts) to include Shadow DOM theming quality as new sub-metrics.

**Current state:** The analyzer only checks:
- CSS property descriptions (35 pts)
- Design token naming patterns (30 pts)  
- CSS parts documentation (35 pts)

**New sub-metrics to add (rebalance weights):**
1. **CSS Parts coverage ratio** (new) — ratio of CSS parts to total internal DOM elements (heuristic from slot count + member count)
2. **Token namespace consistency** (new) — all CSS properties share a common prefix pattern (e.g., all start with --sl-)
3. **Theming completeness** (new) — components that declare CSS properties also declare them for common categories (color, spacing, typography, border-radius)

**Rebalanced weights (still sum to 100 within this dimension):**
- CSS property descriptions: 25 pts (was 35)
- Design token naming: 20 pts (was 30)
- CSS parts documentation: 25 pts (was 35)
- Token namespace consistency: 15 pts (new)
- Theming completeness: 15 pts (new)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Existing tests still pass (no regression on current scores)
- New sub-metrics are exposed in the score_component output
- New fixture data covers components with good/bad theming patterns
- Must pass lint, format, type-check
title: New scoring sub-dimension: Shadow DOM theming quality in CSS Architecture

> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@tests/__fixtures__/benchmark-results/latest-benchmark.json` around lines 1 - > 3353, The JSON fixture latest-benchmark.json is not formatted according to > Prettier; run the repository formatter (e.g. pnpm run format or Prettier > --write) on this fixture and save the normalized JSON so keys like "timestamp", > "scorecards", "comparisonTable" and "performance" follow the project's Prettier > rules (consistent spacing, indentation, trailing commas/line breaks). After > reformatting, re-run pnpm run format:check to confirm the Prettier warning is > resolved and commit the updated file. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@packages/core/src/handlers/analyzers/css-architecture.ts`: - Line 18: The spacing pattern set in the css architecture analyzer currently includes a generic /size/ which matches tokens like `--*-font-size` and causes typography tokens to be counted as spacing; update the spacing entry in the patterns array (the { name: 'spacing', patterns: [...] } object) to remove the generic /size/ and use a more s

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Real correctness bug: `/size/` matches `font-size`, causing typography tokens like `--sdr-font-size` to double-count as spacing. This skews theming completeness scoring. The fix is to remove `/size/` from spacing patterns — the remaining patterns (`/spacing/`, `/padding/`, `/margin/`, `/gap/`) are specific enough. CodeRabbit's suggested regex `/(^|-)size$/i` is actually also wrong (still matches `font-size`), so I'll implement the simpler correct fix of removing the ambiguous pattern.

The `propertyNames` array is computed twice via `cssProperties.map(p => p.name.toLowerCase())` — once inside the `if (cssProperties.length > 0)` block and again for `coveredCategoryNames`. Deduplicating is a correct, low-risk cleanup with no behavioral change.
